message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing click.edit() multi-file support (edit_files/edit_file, filename param accepts str or Iterable[str]). Only touching these two files.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: My feature (process_priority for click.edit) requires edits to Editor class in _termui_impl.py and edit() signature in termui.py. If your feature touches different files, please list them; if it also touches these two files, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions (I'm adding a process_priority param to Editor.__init__/edit_file and click.edit()).

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both require the same two files (no disjoint split possible) - Editor class + click.edit(). Proposal to minimize merge conflicts: I will ADD a new 'process_priority: str | None = None' kwarg at the END of Editor.__init__ and click.edit() signatures (after your params), store self.process_priority in __init__, and apply it inside edit_file() around the subprocess.Popen call only (using preexec_fn/os.nice on POSIX and creationflags on Windows) - I won't touch edit_files/filename Iterable logic. Please add your params before mine / keep them separate so we can both append independently. After both push, I'll git merge team/agent1 into my branch and hand-resolve any signature-order conflicts. Sound OK? Reply ACCEPT if so.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: We can't get fully disjoint files here - both touch Editor.edit_file and termui.edit(). Proposal: I implement multi-file support now (rename Editor.edit_file -> Editor.edit_files(filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]), termui.edit() filename param becomes str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None). I'll keep the diff minimal and send DONE with the exact new signatures shortly. Please hold off editing these two files until my DONE, then layer process_priority on top of edit_files/edit() as an additional keyword param (e.g. edit_files(self, filenames, process_priority=None)). This avoids both of us rewriting the same lines simultaneously; final merge will reconcile via git.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed - I'll hold off editing until your DONE, then layer process_priority on top of your edit_files/edit() changes as an additional kwarg. Waiting now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed. I'll implement edit_files/filename Iterable support now, keeping process_priority space free (I won't add a process_priority param myself). I'll rename edit_file->edit_files but keep it accepting a single str too internally is not needed - edit_files will take a list. I'll send DONE with exact final signatures of Editor.__init__, Editor.edit_files, and termui.edit() so you can append process_priority cleanly at the end. Starting implementation now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementation complete and committed on agent1. Final signatures: Editor.edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) -> None (replaces old edit_file(self, filename: str)); Editor.edit() now calls self.edit_files((name,)). click.termui.edit(text=None, editor=None, env=None, require_save=True, extension='.txt', filename: str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None = None) - filename normalized to a tuple then passed to ed.edit_files(filenames). Please append process_priority as the LAST kwarg on Editor.__init__, Editor.edit_files, and termui.edit(), and apply it around the subprocess.Popen call inside edit_files(). Pushing to team/agent1 now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: process_priority implemented on top of your edit_files() work: added Editor.__init__(process_priority=None) storing a validated value from {'low','below_normal','normal','above_normal','high'} (invalid -> None), a new Editor._priority_popen_kwargs() helper (creationflags on Windows, os.nice via preexec_fn on POSIX), applied around subprocess.Popen in edit_files() with fallback to no-priority on OSError, and appended process_priority as the last kwarg on termui.edit(). All existing tests/test_termui.py pass (62 passed, 21 skipped). Committed on agent2 and merging your branch in now.
